rulings.Case Title: DONNA O'BALLE VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 2, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings brought on the ground that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against it because it owed no duty to plaintiff is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff did not substantively oppose defendant's motion. Plaintiff's request for leave to amend is denied because plaintiff did not demonstrate a reasonable possibility that the defect - lack of duty - can be cured by the proposed twenty-second cause of action. (See Campbell v. Ford Motor Co. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 15; Kesner v. Superior Court (May 15, 2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 251; Haver v. BNSF Railway Co. (June 3, 2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1104.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK L PRATT VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiffs Causes Of Action For Strict Liability, Loss Of Consortium, And The Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 2, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant J.T. Thorpe & Son, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)
